name: Keystone: Autophagosomes and Endolysosomes 2025
desc: Keystone Symposium 'Autophagosomes and Endolysosomes: From Fundamental Mechanisms to Disease Implications', spanning the biogenesis and function of autophagosomes and the endolysosomal system through to disease.
mtor: The lysosomal surface is the physical platform for mTORC1 activation (Rag GTPases, Ragulator) and the TFEB axis; endolysosomal biology is core mechanistic ground for the Atlas.
organizers: Scientific organizers: Christina Towers; Rupert Beale; Leon O. Murphy.
speakers: Organizers/speakers: Christina Towers, Rupert Beale, Leon O. Murphy (full speaker list on the Keystone site).

name: ARDD 2025 — Aging Research and Drug Discovery (12th)
desc: The 12th Aging Research and Drug Discovery meeting — the largest translational geroscience conference — convening academics, biotech founders, pharma and clinicians over a five-day hybrid event on the biology of aging and drug development.
mtor: Rapamycin/mTOR inhibition is one of the central pillars of aging pharmacology; geroscience translation of mTOR is a recurring theme of ARDD and directly informs the Atlas gap/hypothesis layer.
organizers: Organized by the University of Copenhagen (Scheibye-Knudsen group) and partners.
speakers: Speakers/participants included David Sinclair (Harvard), Marco Demaria, Kristen Fortney (BioAge), Morten Meldal (Nobel laureate), Andrew Adams (Eli Lilly), among many others.

name: Keystone: Autophagy — Mechanisms to Therapy 2026
desc: Keystone Symposium covering the roles and mechanisms of autophagy across health and disease, spanning basic, translational and clinical research plus industry developments. Held jointly with the Keystone symposium 'Membrane Dynamics, Repair and Disease' to enable cross-talk.
mtor: mTORC1 is the master negative regulator of autophagy; sessions address mTOR inhibition and nutrient control of autophagic flux. Directly relevant to the autophagy/lysosome arm of the Atlas.
organizers: Scientific organizers: Anne Simonsen; Sharon A. Tooze; Dan Baird.
speakers: Organizers: Anne Simonsen, Sharon A. Tooze, Dan Baird. Keynotes: Erika L. Holzbaur, Harald Stenmark. Invited/session speakers: Felix Randow, Oliver Florey, Jayanta Debnath, Rupert Beale, Maho Hamasaki, Zvulun Elazar, J. Wade Harper, Phyllis Hanson, James H. Hurley, Claudio Bussi, Ivan Dikic, Ai Yamamoto, Joseph D. Mancias, Anastasia Henry, Chris Shoemaker, Simon Wilkinson, Michael Lazarou, Chantell S. Evans, Martin Graef.

name: Keystone: Mitochondria Signaling in Physiology and Disease 2026
desc: Keystone Symposium on mitochondria as signaling organelles that control cellular and organismal physiology, and how mitochondrial dysfunction drives disease.
mtor: Mitochondrial metabolism and nutrient status are tightly integrated with mTORC1 signaling; mitochondrial cues feed into growth control central to the Atlas.
organizers: Scientific organizers: Navdeep S. Chandel; Aleksandra Trifunovic.
speakers: Organizers: Navdeep S. Chandel, Aleksandra Trifunovic. Keynote: Anu Suomalainen Wartiovaara. Invited/session speakers: Elena Ziviani, Kivanc Birsoy, Dipayan Chaudhuri, Dustin Hancks, Xin Jie Chen, Phillip West, María Mittelbrunn, Lena Pernas, Michelle Clasquin, Andrew G. Dillin, Jose Antonio Enríquez, Peter M. Douglas, Dave Pagliarini, Prashant Mishra, Reuben J. Shaw, Jan Riemer, Marcia C. Haigis, Jay H. Chung, Janine Santos, Liron Bar-Peled, Mike P. Murphy.

name: Gordon Research Conference: Autophagy in Stress, Development and Disease 2026
desc: GRC on core autophagy machinery and its roles in cancer, aging, neurodegeneration, infectious disease and development.
mtor: mTORC1 is the master negative regulator of autophagy; this GRC directly covers the mechanistic and disease biology of the autophagy pathway central to the Atlas's autophagy/lysosome arm.

name: AGE 54th Annual Meeting 2026 (American Aging Association)
desc: 54th Annual Meeting of the American Aging Association (AGE). Theme: 'Geroscience: From Discovery to Application.'
mtor: Flagship US geroscience meeting; nutrient-sensing / mTOR biology and rapamycin-class interventions are recurring core topics.
organizers: American Aging Association (AGE). Named lectures: Denham Harman Research Award (Steven Austad); AFAR/Cristofalo Award (Marianna Sadagurski); James Joseph/Mark Smith Award (Alberto Espay).
speakers: Program speakers/chairs incl. Steven Austad (Denham Harman Award Lecture), Rafael de Cabo, Andrzej Bartke, Holly Brown-Borg, Alessandro Bitto (“mTOR inhibition bypasses loss of Complex I activity”), Vishwa Deep Dixit, Marianna Sadagurski (Cristofalo Award), Michael Olivier, Michal Masternak, Liou Sun, Nathan Basisty, Stacy Hussong, Kevin Koronowski, Sarah Ocanas, Cristal Hill. Full program (10 sessions): https://www.americanagingassociation.org/assets/images/2026Meeting/AGE%20Program%20060126.pdf

name: Keystone: Tumor Metabolism — New Frontiers in Diet, Microbiome and Beyond 2026
desc: Keystone Symposium exploring the reciprocal relationship between cancer and whole-body metabolism, including diet, the microbiome, aging and metabolic disease.
mtor: The PI3K–AKT–mTOR axis is a central controller of tumor cell growth and metabolism; diet/nutrient inputs that shape tumor metabolism act largely through mTORC1.
organizers: Scientific organizers: Karen H. Vousden; Cynthia L. Sears; Eileen White.
speakers: Organizers: Karen H. Vousden, Cynthia L. Sears, Eileen White. Keynote: Heather Christofk. Invited/session speakers: Craig B. Thompson, Matthew G. Vander Heiden, Christopher Halbrook, Bertrand Routy, Saman Maleki, Navdeep S. Chandel, Ayelet Erez, Peter J. Mullen, Ralph J. DeBerardinis, Scott J. Dixon, Richard L. Possemato, Lydia Lynch, Marcus D. Goncalves, Thales Papagiannakopoulos, Mariia Yuneva, Oliver Maddocks, Yanxiang (Jessie) Guo.

name: Gordon Research Conference: Systems Aging 2026
desc: Gordon Research Conference on Systems Aging — systems-level approaches to the biology of aging across species, evolution, reproduction, human longevity and frailty. A companion Gordon Research Seminar (GRS) ran June 13-14, 2026.
mtor: Nutrient-sensing and mTOR / insulin-IGF signaling are central pillars of systems-level aging biology featured at this meeting; directly relevant to the Atlas's mTOR-aging axis.

name: Prague Metabolism and Signaling Symposium 2026
venue: National Library of Technology (NTK), Campus Dejvice
desc: Meeting bringing together experts at the intersection of metabolism and signal transduction. ~30 invited speakers plus two keynotes, with short talks and poster sessions. Chaired by David Sabatini (IOCB Prague/Boston) and Katerina Rohlenova. A dedicated closing 'mTOR Biology' session features Brendan Manning, Kuang Shen, Mario Pende and David Sabatini, with Michael N. Hall (the discoverer of TOR) giving the second keynote.
mtor: Highest relevance in the calendar. Co-chaired by David Sabatini, keynote by Michael N. Hall (discovered TOR), and an explicit 'mTOR Biology' session. Many speakers are core mTOR/metabolism labs, several of whom are authors in the Atlas corpus.
organizers: Chairs: Katerina Rohlenova (Institute of Biotechnology, Czech Academy of Sciences); David Sabatini (IOCB Prague & IOCB Boston). Keynotes: Karen Vousden (Francis Crick Institute); Michael N. Hall (Biozentrum, University of Basel).
speakers: Keynotes: Karen Vousden, Michael N. Hall. Invited: Janelle Ayres, Liron Bar-Peled, Issam Ben Sahra, Kivanc Birsoy, Anne Brunet, Ralph DeBerardinis, Andrew Dillin, Alejo Efeyan, Mark Febbraio, Lydia Finley, Gerta Hoxhaj, Navdeep Chandel, Heather Christofk, Nada Kalaany, Naama Kanarek, Pekka Katajisto, Brendan Manning, Michael Pacold, Thales Papagiannakopoulos, Erika Pearce, Mario Pende, Richard Possemato, Jared Rutter, Luca Scorrano, Reuben Shaw, Kuang Shen, Celeste Simon, Jessica Spinelli, Eileen White, Omer Yilmaz.

name: ARDD 2026 — Aging Research and Drug Discovery (13th)
desc: The 13th Aging Research and Drug Discovery meeting, confirmed for late August / early September 2026 in Copenhagen — continuing the flagship translational geroscience series.
mtor: As with prior editions, rapamycin/mTOR-based interventions are a core geroscience topic; a key future venue to track for mTOR translational work.
organizers: University of Copenhagen (Scheibye-Knudsen group) and partners.
speakers: Speaker list to be announced (dates: late Aug / early Sep 2026).

name: CSHL: Mechanisms of Aging 2026
desc: CSHL meeting on the regulation and function of genes and processes known or suspected to control cellular and organismal aging.
mtor: Nutrient-sensing longevity pathways (TOR/AMPK/insulin-IGF) are central; organizer William Mair studies TOR/AMPK control of aging in C. elegans — directly adjacent to the Atlas.
organizers: Organizers: Rozalyn Anderson (Univ. Wisconsin–Madison); William Mair (Harvard T.H. Chan); Ashley Webb (Buck Institute).
speakers: Keynote: Rafael de Cabo (NIA). Confirmed invited speakers: Joseph Baur, Andreas Beyer, Jens Brüning, Weiwei Dang, Jennifer Elisseeff, Judith Frydman, Vadim Gladyshev, Margaret Goodell, Catherine Kaczorowski, Caroline Kumsta, Dudley Lamming, Jodi Nunnari, Courtney Marie Peterson, Marissa Schafer, Ming Xu. Organizers: Rozalyn Anderson, William Mair, Ashley Webb.

name: Cell Symposia: Hallmarks of Aging 2026
desc: Cell Press Symposium exploring the cellular and molecular hallmarks of aging — from metabolism and disease to gerotechnology and gerotherapeutics.
mtor: Deregulated nutrient sensing (mTOR, AMPK, IIS) is a canonical hallmark of aging; organizers include caloric-restriction (de Cabo) and autophagy (Cuervo) leaders whose work overlaps mTOR biology.
organizers: Organizers: Rafael de Cabo (NIA); Ana Maria Cuervo (Albert Einstein); with Cell Press editors. Keynote: Luigi Ferrucci (NIA).
speakers: Keynote Luigi Ferrucci; confirmed speakers include Berenice Benayoun, Haim Cohen, Dolores Corella, Clara Correia-Melo, Vishwa Deep Dixit, David Glass, Mari-Carmen Gomez-Cabrera, among others.

name: 2026 Biomarkers of Aging Conference
venue: Joseph B. Martin Conference Center, Harvard Medical School
desc: Annual conference from the Biomarkers of Aging Consortium convening geroscience researchers to advance standardized, clinically validated biomarkers of aging and assess longevity intervention outcomes.
mtor: Broad geroscience/biomarkers meeting; several speakers (Gladyshev, Verdin, Rando, Gorbunova, Horvath) work on nutrient-sensing and metabolic longevity pathways adjacent to the Atlas's mTOR-aging axis, though the program itself is not mTOR-specific.
organizers: Conference Chairs: Vadim Gladyshev, Mahdi Moqri, Chiara Herzog, Andrea Cipriano, Jesse Poganik.
speakers: Omar Abudayyeh, Maxim Artyomov, Dan Belsky, Heike Bischoff-Ferrari, Martin Borch Jensen, Ed Boyden, Peter Fedichev, Jennifer Garrison, Vadim Gladyshev, Vera Gorbunova, Clare Gustafson, Steve Horvath, Michael Levin, João Pedro de Magalhães, Samantha Morris, Thomas Rando, Birgit Schilling, Vittorio Sebastiano, Mike Snyder, Eric Verdin.

name: Fifth Geroscience Summit: Revisiting the Geroscience Hypothesis - Focus on Health
desc: NIA-hosted summit (with the NIH Geroscience Interest Group) bringing together academic and industry researchers, clinicians, and other stakeholders in geroscience across 8 sessions to assess the state of the science, identify gaps, and build a 3-5 year research roadmap focused on healthspan.
mtor: NIH/NIA's flagship cross-institute geroscience summit. Gerotherapeutic development, hallmarks-of-aging biology, and healthspan biomarker sessions directly track the Atlas's nutrient-sensing/mTOR-aging axis; Session V (Gerotherapeutics for Health, Clinical Trials) and Session VII (New Initiatives in Geroscience) are the most mTOR/rapamycin-adjacent.
organizers: NIH/NIA Geroscience Interest Group (GSIG). Welcome: Jay Bhattacharya (NIH Director), Richard Hodes (NIA Director). Session chairs: Juan Carlos Izpisua Belmonte, Jesse Poganik, Eileen Crimmins, John Beard, John Newman, Siobhan Malany, Jodi Nunnari, Elizabeth Necka. Overall moderators: Felipe Sierra, Vadim Gladyshev, Tony Wyss-Coray.
speakers: Keynote: Luigi Ferrucci. Session I: Juan Carlos Izpisua Belmonte, Thomas Rando, George Kuchel, Guanghui Liu. Session II: Jesse Poganik, Yousin Suh, Paola Sebastiani. Session III: Eileen Crimmins, Cathal McCrory, Jessica Faul, Dana Goldman, Andrew Scott. Session IV: Daniel Belsky, Jennifer Brach, Su-In Lee, Michael Snyder. Session V: John Newman, Heinrich Jasper, James Peyer, Brianna Stubbs. Session VI: Warren Casey, Kristen Brennand, Joseph Wu, Luiz Eduardo Bertassoni, Francesca Duncan. Session VII: Jamie Justice, Joan Mannick, Andrew Brack. Session VIII: TBD. Roundtable participant: Steven Austad.

name: Keystone Symposia: Autophagy in Health and Disease — From Mechanisms to Therapeutics 2027
desc: Keystone Symposium (joint with 'Induced Proximity and Targeted Protein Degradation') covering core autophagy machinery, selective autophagy, non-canonical autophagy pathways, and therapeutic exploitation of autophagy including AUTACs.
mtor: mTORC1 is the principal negative regulator of autophagy initiation; sessions on lysosomal integration of biosynthetic/catabolic signaling (Zoncu) and autophagy in aging (Boya, Simon) are directly relevant to the Atlas's mTOR-autophagy-aging axis.
organizers: Scientific Organizers: Hong Zhang, Susan Ferro-Novick, Hitoshi Nakatogawa.
speakers: Keynote: Noboru Mizushima. Invited: Hitoshi Nakatogawa, Sascha Martens, Nobuo N. Noda, Susan Ferro-Novick, Florian Wilfling, Eric H. Baehrecke, Ana Maria Cuervo, Hong Zhang, Xiaochen Wang, Roberto Zoncu, Vojo Deretic, Yan Zhao, Mondira Kundu, Dimitri Krainc, Patricia Boya, Ai Yamamoto, Anna Katharina Simon, Boxun Lu, Vassiliki Nikoletopoulou.

name: Keystone Symposia: Healthy Aging & Therapeutic Opportunities 2027
desc: Keystone Symposia conference examining emerging strategies for promoting healthy aging, with a focus on biomarkers of aging, therapeutic development, and interventions to improve quality of life. Sessions cover geromedicine, biomarkers of aging, non-pharmacologic approaches, combination therapies, drug target identification, and translational/regulatory challenges.
mtor: Co-organized by Brian K. Kennedy, a leading rapamycin/mTOR and NAD+ longevity researcher; geromedicine and combination-therapy sessions are directly adjacent to the Atlas's mTOR-intervention focus.
organizers: Scientific Organizers: Derek Huffman (Albert Einstein College of Medicine); Kristen Fortney (BioAge Labs); Brian K. Kennedy (National University of Singapore); Shu-Chun Teng (National Taiwan University).
speakers: Confirmed speaker roster not yet published as of July 2026; organizers listed above.

name: World Aging & Longevity Conference 2026
desc: International conference bringing together researchers and clinicians focused on the biology of aging and longevity, covering geroscience, age-related disease mechanisms, and translational interventions to extend healthspan.
mtor: Direct coverage of mTOR/rapamycin signalling as a longevity pathway; sessions on geroscience interventions, autophagy regulation, and mechanistic aging biology.
